expected_findings.summary: 2 1099-B lot(s) missing Code W on Form 8949 (disallowed loss $3,030)
expected_findings.detail: The broker reported a wash sale loss disallowed amount on these lots, but Form 8949 column (f) does not carry Code W and column (g) does not add the disallowance back. IRC §1091 requires the disallowed loss to be tracked and added to the replacement-lot basis. This is a mechanical compliance error that routinely triggers IRS CP2000 notices because the IRS wash-sale computer match sees the broker-reported amount and expects it on the return.
Form 8889 requires line 2 (contributions), line 6 (allowable), and line 13 (deduction) to reconcile with the TY2025 statutory limit. line 6 is $8,300 but TY2025 family limit is $8,550.

expected_findings.summary: Passive K-1 loss from Synthetic Partners 17 LP must be suspended
expected_findings.detail: The K-1 is marked passive and has a loss. Per IRC §469 passive losses can only offset passive income. If there is no passive income to absorb the loss this year, it must be suspended on Form 8582 and carried forward.

expected_findings.summary: PTET election can effectively uncap the SALT deduction on K-1 income
expected_findings.detail: IRS Notice 2020-75 blessed state-level PTET regimes as a way to pay state tax at the entity level, converting a capped SALT deduction into a federal ordinary business expense that passes through as lower K-1 income. Consider the PTET election with each state where the K-1 generates income.

expected_findings.summary: Avery has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Avery is 36 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.

expected_findings.summary: Riley has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Riley is 36 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.
Avery has contributed $14,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $9,500 can still be deferred before year end.
Riley has contributed $6,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $17,500 can still be deferred before year end.

expected_findings.summary: Illinois PTET election available on pass-through income
expected_findings.detail: Illinois allows a pass-through entity to elect to pay state tax at the entity level, producing a federal ordinary business deduction that effectively uncaps the SALT cost on this K-1. The return has eligible pass-through income but the state slice is not flagged as having made the election.

expected_findings.summary: California PTET election available on pass-through income
expected_findings.detail: California allows a pass-through entity to elect to pay state tax at the entity level, producing a federal ordinary business deduction that effectively uncaps the SALT cost on this K-1. The return has eligible pass-through income but the state slice is not flagged as having made the election.

expected_findings.summary: Pass-through income allocated across 2+ PTET states
expected_findings.detail: Multiple states allow a PTET election but apportion the deduction differently. Consult the partnership's state apportionment schedule before electing — the federal benefit stacks per state.

expected_findings.summary: Need $25,904 withheld to hit 90% current year safe harbor
expected_findings.detail: The alternative safe harbor is 90% of current year tax. This is usually only easier to hit than 110% prior year if income dropped.
